meta.home.title: verba — Ukrainian proverbs and sayings
meta.home.desc: A corpus of Ukrainian proverbs and sayings — 48,787 entries from five collections (1841–present), with search by theme, source, and meaning.
meta.home.ogDesc: A corpus of 48,787 Ukrainian proverbs and sayings with search by theme, source, and meaning.

about.title: About — verba
about.desc: About verba — an open corpus of 48,787 Ukrainian proverbs and sayings from five collections (1841–present): sources, methodology, license, and citation.
about.lede1: verba is an open, unified corpus of Ukrainian proverbs, adages, and sayings, compiled from five digitized collections spanning 1841 to the present.
about.lede2: {count} entries; each with a modern spelling and thematic tags, searchable by word, theme, source, and meaning.
about.s1.p1: For decades, Ukrainian paremiology remained scattered across individual printed collections with varying spelling conventions and no shared machine-readable format. verba unifies these collections into one corpus, making it searchable, researchable, and reusable.
about.s1.p2: Each entry includes: the original text (preserving the source's historical spelling), a modern spelling, 1–3 themes from a fixed set of 27, the source and reference, and a variant group (linking probable dialectal equivalents).
about.s3.p1: Historical editions were digitized; OCR (tesseract) was applied to scans, while Nomys material was extracted from a critical edition. Duplicates were soft-merged, and probable dialectal variants were linked rather than removed. Texts are presented as close to the sources as possible.
about.s3.note: Honest limitations: the Nomys collection (1864) represents the best possible OCR (≈75–80% character accuracy), so occasional recognition errors occur. Modern spellings and themes were AI-generated and are approximate (modern spelling ≈95% acceptable, themes ≈85%). The primary theme tag is most reliable.
about.s3.p2: In the data itself, typography is reduced to plain ASCII (straight quotes, apostrophe, hyphen) for easy programmatic processing; Ukrainian typography (« », em dash, ') is applied only for display — on the website and cards.
about.s4.li1: Web app — search, filters by theme and source, semantic search "by meaning", Browse mode, and saved proverbs.
about.s4.li2: REST API (/api/v1) — JSON · JSONL · XML · CSV · TSV; see the API documentation.
about.s4.li3: Download — the full corpus in every format, along with a data card and Croissant metadata, in GitHub releases and as a dataset on Hugging Face.
about.s5.p1: Curation and enrichment (corpus structure, modern spellings, themes, cleaned annotations, variant groups) — CC BY 4.0. Historical texts (1841–1909) — public domain. Modern collections (Bobkova; Mlodzynskyi, 2009) remain under publisher rights; included for research and education, with attribution via the sources field.
api.title: API — Ukrainian proverbs
api.desc: REST API for the Ukrainian proverbs corpus — documentation, parameters, and curl examples.
api.standfirst: REST API for the Ukrainian proverbs corpus — JSON, JSONL, XML, CSV, and TSV.
api.intro: Base path: https://verbacorpus.org/api/v1. All endpoints are public, CORS is open (*), no key required.
api.negotiateNote: Response format is selected via the ?format=json|jsonl|xml|csv|tsv parameter or the Accept header (e.g. Accept: application/x-ndjson). Default is json. Collection endpoints return an X-Total-Count header with the total result count.
api.s.search.desc: Full-text and semantic search across the corpus.
api.s.browse.desc: Filtering, random entries, and bulk export.
api.s.schema.desc: Structure of the Proverb object in JSON/JSONL/XML responses.
api.ep.search.desc: Searches proverbs by keyword (MiniSearch). Without q returns the full corpus with pagination.
api.ep.semantic.desc: Embeds the query via BGE-M3 and finds the nearest proverbs via Vectorize. Results include a score field (0–1).
api.ep.random.desc: Returns one or more random proverbs, optionally filtered.
api.ep.query.desc: Filters the corpus by theme, source, variant group, or presence of explanation. Without parameters returns all entries with pagination.
api.ep.proverb.desc: Returns a single record along with its explanation (if available).
api.ep.export.desc: Returns the full corpus (or a filtered subset) in a single request. Includes the explanation field. CSV or TSV is recommended for large downloads.
api.ep.categories.desc: Returns an array of {key, label, count} objects for all thematic categories.
api.ep.meta.desc: Full statistics: total count, breakdown by sources and themes, build date.
api.param.name: Parameter
api.schema.csvNote: CSV and TSV include the same fields as strings; arrays are comma-separated. explanation only appears in /export and /proverb/{id}.
